back: Linear Algebra is a branch of mathematics concerned with vectors, vector spaces, linear transformations, and systems of linear equations. It provides tools for solving problems involving linear relationships.
A vector is a mathematical object that has both magnitude and direction. In n-dimensional space, it is represented as an ordered tuple of n numbers (v₁, v₂, ..., vₙ).

A vector space is a collection of vectors that can be added together and multiplied by scalars (real or complex numbers), satisfying eight axioms including closure, commutativity, associativity, and existence of additive identity and inverses.
A linear transformation is a mapping T: V → W between two vector spaces that preserves vector addition and scalar multiplication, i.e., T(u + v) = T(u) + T(v) and T(αv) = αT(v).
A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns. An m×n matrix has m rows and n columns, and is used to represent linear transformations and systems of linear equations.

front: What is the dot product of two vectors?
back: The dot product (or inner product) of two vectors u = (u₁, u₂, ..., uₙ) and v = (v₁, v₂, ..., vₙ) is u·v = u₁v₁ + u₂v₂ + ... + uₙvₙ, resulting in a scalar.

front: What is the cross product of two vectors?
back: The cross product of two vectors in ℝ³ results in a vector perpendicular to both, given by u × v = (u₂v₃ - u₃v₂, u₃v₁ - u₁v₃, u₁v₂ - u₂v₁).
A set of vectors is linearly independent if no vector in the set can be expressed as a linear combination of the others. Formally, if c₁v₁ + c₂v₂ + ... + cₙvₙ = 0 implies all coefficients cᵢ = 0.

front: What is the span of a set of vectors?
back: The span of a set of vectors is the set of all possible linear combinations of those vectors. It forms a subspace containing all vectors reachable by scaling and adding the original vectors.

front: Define a basis of a vector space.
back: A basis is a set of linearly independent vectors that span the entire vector space. The number of vectors in the basis is called the dimension of the vector space.

front: What is a system of linear equations?
back: A system of linear equations is a collection of two or more linear equations involving the same variables. The solution is the set of values that satisfy all equations simultaneously.
Matrix multiplication of an m×n matrix A and n×p matrix B produces an m×p matrix C where Cᵢⱼ = Σₖ Aᵢₖ × Bₖⱼ (sum over k from 1 to n).

front: Define the transpose of a matrix.
back: The transpose of a matrix A (denoted Aᵀ) is obtained by swapping rows and columns. The element at position (i,j) in A becomes (j,i) in Aᵀ.
The determinant is a scalar value computed from a square matrix that encodes properties of the linear transformation. A non-zero determinant indicates the matrix is invertible.
For a square matrix A, a non-zero vector v is an eigenvector if A v = λ v for some scalar λ (the eigenvalue). The eigenvector direction remains unchanged by the linear transformation.
